tickets.title: US001: Browse and order food from mobile app
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to browse the cafeteria menu and place an order from my phone, so that I can skip the queue and pick up my food without waiting in line.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Student can view the full menu with item names, prices, and descriptions
- [ ] Student can add items to cart and confirm order from mobile app
- [ ] Student receives a confirmation notification after order is placed

tickets.title: US002: Filter menu by allergens
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to filter the menu by allergens, so that I can hide items containing ingredients I am allergic to and order safely.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Student can select one or more allergens to filter (e.g., peanuts, gluten, dairy)
- [ ] Menu items containing selected allergens are completely hidden from view
- [ ] Filter settings are saved for future sessions

tickets.title: US003: Receive discount push notification
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to receive a push notification at 11:30 AM with a discount on the daily special, so that I can take advantage of promotions and save money on my lunch.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Student receives a push notification at 11:30 AM on weekdays
- [ ] Notification includes the daily special name and discount amount
- [ ] Student can tap the notification to go directly to the item in the app
- [ ] Student can opt out of notifications in settings

tickets.title: US004: Mark menu item as Sold Out
tickets.description:
As an Administrator, I want to instantly mark a menu item as Sold Out, so that students cannot place orders for unavailable items and the kitchen avoids confusion.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Administrator can mark any item as Sold Out from the kitchen display
- [ ] Sold Out items are immediately hidden or disabled on the student app
- [ ] Administrator can restore an item to available status at any time
- [ ] Changes take effect in under 5 seconds after confirmation

tickets.title: US005: View incoming orders on kitchen display
tickets.description:
As an Administrator, I want to view all incoming orders on a kitchen display screen, so that the kitchen staff can prepare orders efficiently and reduce wait times.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] Kitchen display shows all incoming orders in real time
- [ ] Orders are displayed with student name, items, and order time
- [ ] Display continues to function offline for at least 10 minutes if Wi-Fi cuts out
- [ ] Orders placed during offline period sync automatically when connection is restored

tickets.title: NFR Epic 1: Reliability: The kitchen display system must be able to work offline f
tickets.description: Reliability: The kitchen display system must be able to work offline for at least 10 minutes without crashing and automatically sync all orders once the internet connection is restored.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 2: Performance: Menu item availability must update in real time so that w
tickets.description: Performance: Menu item availability must update in real time so that when a Chef marks an item as "Sold Out", it is immediately reflected on the student app to prevent further orders.

tickets.title: NFR Epic 3: Usability: The app must support allergen filtering so that students ca
tickets.description: Usability: The app must support allergen filtering so that students can hide menu items containing specific ingredients, ensuring a safe and anxiety-free browsing experience.

analysis.ambiguity1:
Requirement: "I want students to order food on their phone while they are walking from class."

Why vague: There is no defined cutoff time for when orders can be placed before pickup. It is also unclear whether the app requires a stable internet connection to place orders or supports offline ordering for students.
analysis.ambiguity2:
Requirement: "We want to send push notifications to students at 11:30 AM offering them a discount on the daily special."

Why vague: There is no definition of what qualifies as a "discount" — is it a fixed amount or a percentage? It is also unclear whether all students receive the notification or only those who have opted in, and whether the 11:30 AM time is fixed or configurable.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Conflict: The IT Infrastructure Team states the kitchen display system must work offline for at least 10 minutes without crashing. However, the Chef needs to mark items as "Sold Out" instantly so students stop ordering unavailable items in real time.

Solution: Implement a local cache on the kitchen display system that stores and processes incoming orders offline for up to 10 minutes. When the Chef marks an item as "Sold Out", the update is stored locally and synced to the student app immediately when connection is restored. Additionally, display a warning on the student app when the menu was last synced more than 2 minutes ago.
analysis.nfr1: Reliability: The kitchen display system must be able to work offline for at least 10 minutes without crashing and automatically sync all orders once the internet connection is restored.
analysis.nfr2: Performance: Menu item availability must update in real time so that when a Chef marks an item as "Sold Out", it is immediately reflected on the student app to prevent further orders.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: The app must support allergen filtering so that students can hide menu items containing specific ingredients, ensuring a safe and anxiety-free browsing experience.
